title: PaperAnalysis
description: Closed JSON Schema for the output of each arXiv paper analysis. One record per paper, produced by the analyzer pipeline (MiniMax M2.7) and validated by the Pydantic model at src/ai_ideator/analyzer/schema.py.

tags.description: 5-15 lowercase-hyphenated free-form tags summarizing the paper's themes.

characteristics.description: Queryable dimensions the paper exposes. The spine for cross-paper comparison and ranking.

novelty.description: Concrete novelty claims. What is genuinely new vs. prior work.

open_problems.description: Limitations, open questions, or future work directions identified in the paper.

Characteristic.title: Characteristic
Characteristic.description: One measurable dimension the paper exposes (e.g. 'attack_success_rate', 'throughput_qps', 'bleu_score'). Dimensions are named freely by the LLM and canonicalized in a separate index step.

dimension.description: Short canonical name in snake_case (e.g. 'attack_success_rate', 'throughput_qps'). Cross-paper canonical — synonyms are clustered in the index step.

what_it_measures.description: One sentence: what this dimension represents.

unit.description: Unit of measurement (%, ms, bits, queries, qubits, tokens, etc.) or empty string if dimensionless.

direction.description: Whether higher values are better, lower are better, or it's context-dependent.

value.description: Best human-readable value as stated in the paper (e.g. '92.4%', '3.2x faster', 'comparable to GPT-4').

value_numeric.description: Numeric value if cleanly extractable from the paper. null when the value is qualitative or parametric.

value_class.description: Bucket classifying how precise the value is. 'numeric' = exact number; 'parametric' = depends on parameter; 'qualitative-strong' = clear directional claim; 'qualitative-weak' = hedged claim; 'asymptotic' = big-O or limit behavior.

vs_baseline.description: Comparison statement against prior work (e.g. '12pp better than X', 'matches SOTA on Y benchmark').

evidence.description: How the value is supported: 'theoretical proof', 'benchmark on N tasks', 'ablation study', 'case study', 'human evaluation', etc.

confidence.description: Confidence in the claimed value. 'high' = rigorous evaluation, multiple benchmarks; 'medium' = solid but limited evaluation; 'low' = preliminary or anecdotal.

context.description: Conditions/scope under which the value holds (e.g. 'on Llama-3-8B', 'English only', 'in honest-majority setting').

Topic.title: Topic
Topic.description: What/how/why framing of the paper's contribution.

what.description: One sentence: what the paper does.

how.description: One sentence: the technical mechanism or approach.

why_matters.description: One sentence: why a builder/practitioner should care.

good_for.description: Use cases where this approach shines.

not_for.description: Cases where it fails, is inappropriate, or doesn't apply.

Classification.title: Classification
Classification.description: Paper categorization along research-type and maturity axes.

research_type.description: Loose label for the kind of research. Canonicalized in the index step. Common values: 'theoretical', 'empirical', 'systems', 'survey', 'benchmark', 'position'.

contribution_type.description: What the paper contributes. Common values: 'algorithm', 'benchmark', 'framework', 'dataset', 'model', 'technique', 'tool', 'analysis'.

domain.description: Top-level research subfield (e.g. 'computational creativity', 'NLP', 'multi-agent systems', 'knowledge representation').

Meta.title: Meta
Meta.description: Provenance metadata. Populated by the analyzer runner, NOT by the LLM. Records when, how, and at what cost the analysis was produced.

analyzed_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp (UTC) when the analysis was produced (e.g. '2026-05-01T12:34:56+00:00').

model.description: Model identifier used for the analysis (e.g. 'MiniMax-M2.7').

input_kind.description: 'full_text' if the analyzer received >2500 chars of paper body; 'abstract_only' otherwise.

input_chars.description: Character count of the text sent to the LLM.

latency_s.description: Wall-clock time for the LLM call in seconds.
